title: Lesson 3 – Places and Prices
description: Ortswörter ここ/そこ/あそこ, N は Place です, どこ (wo), いくら (wie viel), Zähler.

grammar.title: ここ/そこ/あそこ (Demonstrative Place Words)
grammar.structure: ここ / そこ / あそこ は N です
grammar.explanation: ここ refers to the place where the speaker is. そこ refers to the place where the listener is. あそこ refers to a place far from both the speaker and the listener. These are used to indicate locations. どこ is the interrogative word for asking about places.
grammar.example_sentences:
① ここは 教室です。
  (Koko wa kyoushitsu desu.)
  — Here is the classroom.
② そこは 食堂です。
  (Soko wa shokudou desu.)
  — There is the cafeteria.
③ トイレは どこですか。
  (Toire wa doko desu ka.)
  — Where is the restroom?

grammar.title: N は Place です (Location of Things/People)
grammar.explanation: This pattern indicates where something or someone is located. The topic (N) is followed by は, and the place is given before です. The polite forms こちら, そちら, あちら, and どちら can be used instead of ここ, そこ, あそこ, and どこ.
grammar.example_sentences:
④ 受付は 一階です。
  (Uketsuke wa ikkai desu.)
  — The reception is on the first floor.
⑤ 山田さんの 会社は どちらですか。
  (Yamada-san no kaisha wa dochira desu ka.)
  — Where is Mr. Yamada's company?

grammar.title: N はいくらですか (Asking Prices)
grammar.structure: N は いくら ですか
grammar.explanation: いくら is used to ask the price of something. The answer uses the counter 円 (en, yen). Numbers are combined with 円 to state prices.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑥ この 時計は いくらですか。
  (Kono tokei wa ikura desu ka.)
  — How much is this watch?
…三千円です。(Sanzen en desu.) — It's 3,000 yen.
⑦ その ネクタイは いくらですか。
  (Sono nekutai wa ikura desu ka.)
  — How much is that necktie?
…二千五百円です。(Nisen gohyaku en desu.) — It's 2,500 yen.

grammar.title: 何階 (Floor Counter)
grammar.structure: N は なんがい ですか
grammar.explanation: 何階 (nangai) is used to ask which floor something is on. The counter ～階 (kai/gai) is used for floors of a building. Note the sound changes: 三階 is sankai (not sangai), and 何階 is nangai.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑧ 食堂は 何階ですか。
  (Shokudou wa nangai desu ka.)
  — What floor is the cafeteria on?
…地下一階です。(Chika ikkai desu.) — It's on basement level one.

conversation.title: Where is the cafeteria?
conversation.title_ja: しょくどうは どこですか

lines.speaker: Kimura
lines.japanese: すみません。食堂は どこですか。
lines.romaji: Sumimasen. Shokudou wa doko desu ka.
lines.english: Excuse me. Where is the cafeteria?

lines.speaker: Hayashi
lines.english: The cafeteria is in the basement.

lines.speaker: Kimura
lines.japanese: そうですか。トイレも 地下ですか。
lines.romaji: Sou desu ka. Toire mo chika desu ka.
lines.english: I see. Is the restroom also in the basement?

lines.speaker: Hayashi
lines.japanese: いいえ、トイレは 二階です。
lines.english: No, the restroom is on the second floor.

lines.speaker: Kimura
lines.japanese: 会議室は 何階ですか。
lines.english: What floor is the conference room on?

lines.speaker: Hayashi
lines.japanese: 会議室は 三階です。エレベーターは あそこです。
lines.romaji: Kaigishitsu wa sankai desu. Erebeetaa wa asoko desu.
lines.english: The conference room is on the third floor. The elevator is over there.

lines.speaker: Kimura
lines.japanese: ありがとうございます。
